steps.title: Read for the signal
steps.content: The sentence structure gives a reason introduced by “because”: two Osage women were foremost twentieth-century ballet dancers. When a reason is supplied, the blank must describe a choice that is justified by that reason — not random, not unforeseeable, not emotionally driven.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — a suitable: “Suitable” means appropriate or fitting. The Tallchief sisters’ prominence gives a clear reason ballet is fitting for Osage stories. ✓
  • Choice B — a determined: “Determined” implies overcoming opposition or difficulty. The text describes no obstacle Tinker-Smith had to overcome — this word is unsupported. ✗
  • Choice C — an arbitrary: “Arbitrary” means random or without reason. The passage explicitly supplies a reason, so arbitrary directly contradicts the passage’s logic. ✗
  • Choice D — an unpredictable: “Unpredictable” means surprising or unexpected. Nothing in the passage frames the choice as a surprise; the Tallchief connection makes ballet a natural fit. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
